Title: The combined effects of Golden2-like 2 and Pseudo-Response Regulator 2 control the fruit pigmentation in pepper

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Numex halloween; Habanero orange; Royal black; IT158782 and other accessions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function psy1 contributes to orange/peach/white ripe fruit by reducing carotenoid accumulation; reduced PSY1 expression in GLK2/PRR2 dual-variation backgrounds is associated with lower capsanthin and carotenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural psy1 mutations were part of the genotype combinations explaining ripe fruit color diversity in Capsicum accessions. 2. In GLK2/PRR2 dual-variation backgrounds, PSY1 transcript abundance was consistently reduced. 3. Reduced PSY1 expression was associated with lower capsanthin and carotenoid accumulation in ripe fruit.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies committed precursor pool for downstream carotenoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a major control point/dominant role in fruit carotenogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural variant analysis across accessions; qRT-PCR of pathway genes; HPLC carotenoid profiling; gene-silencing genetic interaction context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in ripe fruit; reduced in GLK2/PRR2 dual-variation backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: SPME-GC-MS volatile profiling; HPLC pigment profiling; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of PSY1 reduces nutritionally valuable carotenoids and alters ripe fruit color.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for breeding/engineering ripe fruit carotenoid content and color; should be considered with GLK2/PRR2 and CCS alleles.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PSY1 was not the primary mechanistic focus of experimentation but was repeatedly implicated as the key carotenoid biosynthetic node downstream of GLK2/PRR2.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Numex halloween; Royal black; IT158782 and other accessions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CCS contributes to orange-yellow/lemon-yellow/white ripe fruit; co-silencing of GLK2 and CCS in NH caused white ripe fruits with no detectable carotenoid.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural ccs mutations contributed to ripe color variation among Capsicum accessions. 2. Co-silencing of GLK2 and CCS in NH produced white ripe fruit. 3. Carotenoids were not detected in GLK2-CCS-silenced NH, supporting CCS as essential for red carotenoid end-product accumulation in the proper genetic background.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms terminal red carotenoid products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for red branch, but not established as global rate-limiting step in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural variant analysis; co-silencing with GLK2 by VIGS; HPLC carotenoid profiling; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in ripe fruit; altered in some GLK2/PRR2 perturbation backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: HPLC pigment profiling; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of CCS abolishes/redesigns red carotenoid accumulation and can cause white fruit in sensitized backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering terminal red carotenoid composition and fruit color; allele combinations with GLK2/PRR2/PSY1 require caution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CCS expression did not correlate with color intensity as strongly as PSY1 according to the authors, but CCS loss was decisive for white fruit in the NH co-silencing experiment.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Milyang; Royal black; Numex halloween and diverse accessions depending on allele
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Numex halloween; Royal black; Milyang × Goshiki F2; diverse accessions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Golden2-like GARP transcription factor with DNA-binding domain, proline-rich region, AREAEAA motif, and GCT box
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a complex with PRR2; interaction requires the GCT box
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/positive regulation of plastid development and pigment biosynthesis, likely partly via protein interaction with PRR2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Chlorophyll biosynthesis genes HEMA; CHLH; CRD; DVR; PORB; CAO; CHLM; CHLG; carotenoid biosynthesis genes including PSY1, ZISO, CHY2, ZEP
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulator; dual loss downregulates chlorophyll and carotenoid biosynthesis genes, and GLK2 silencing alone reduces several chlorophyll genes and PSY1 in NH
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Plastid development; chlorophyll biosynthesis; carotenoid biosynthesis; fruit pigmentation; volatile-associated plastid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chlorophyll; carotenoids including lutein, β-carotene, capsanthin; pigmentation-associated volatiles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional quality, fruit color, and sensory quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GLK2 is a major positive regulator of pepper fruit pigmentation; loss or silencing reduces plastid size, thylakoid number, chlorophylls and carotenoids, causing light-green to pale-green immature fruit and peach/less pigmented ripe fruit depending on genetic background.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major regulator of immature fruit pigmentation and an essential determinant of the c1 locus together with PRR2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural glk2 mutations alone generally caused light-green immature fruit, while combined glk2/prr2 caused pale-green fruit with strongly reduced chlorophyll. 2. In NH carrying prr2/psy1, GLK2 silencing caused pale-green immature fruit and peach ripe fruit with reduced plastid development and pigment accumulation. 3. Co-silencing GLK2 and PRR2 in RB caused pale-green immature fruit, and GLK2 physically interacted with PRR2 in the nucleus via the GCT box.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association analysis in 30 accessions; F2 segregation/genotyping; VIGS silencing; TEM plastid ultrastructure; HPLC pigment profiling; qRT-PCR; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during fruit ripening in pepper relative to tomato GLK2 profile discussed by authors
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit-enriched/spatially active in fruits; not visibly affecting leaves when silenced, unlike GLK1
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: SPME-GC-MS volatile profiling; HPLC pigment profiling; transcript comparison using published pepper/tomato datasets; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing GLK2 lowers nutritionally important pigments and alters flavor volatiles; no obvious leaf phenotype from GLK2 silencing in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for improving fruit chlorophyll/carotenoid accumulation and pigmentation; allele status should be combined with PRR2, PSY1, and CCS for predictive breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude GLK2 has a stronger effect than PRR2 on immature fruit color; natural mutant proteins lose the GCT box and interaction capability.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Milyang; Royal black; Numex halloween and diverse accessions depending on allele
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Habanero orange; Royal black; Numex halloween; Milyang × Goshiki F2; diverse accessions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pseudo-response regulator with REC-like domain, GARP DNA-binding domain, proline-rich region, and GCT box
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a complex with GLK2; interaction requires the GCT box
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive transcriptional/regulatory control of plastid development and pigment biosynthesis, acting additively/complementarily with GLK2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Chlorophyll biosynthesis genes CHLM; CHLG and others context-dependently; carotenoid biosynthesis genes PSY1; PDS; ZISO; LCYB1; LCYB2; ZEP; CCS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulator; PRR2 silencing in glk2 background reduced multiple carotenoid biosynthetic genes and some chlorophyll genes; dual loss with GLK2 broadly reduced pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Plastid development; chlorophyll biosynthesis; carotenoid biosynthesis; fruit pigmentation; volatile-associated plastid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Chlorophyll; carotenoids including lutein, β-carotene, capsanthin, zeaxanthin; pigmentation-associated volatiles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional quality, fruit color, and sensory quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PRR2 is a positive regulator that acts with GLK2; silencing in a glk2 background causes pale-green immature fruit and peach ripe fruit with reduced plastid development and pigment content, while single PRR2 perturbation may show weaker effects depending on background.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Secondary/additive but necessary determinant of the c1 locus together with GLK2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Natural prr2 alone did not always reduce pigmentation strongly, but combined glk2/prr2 consistently caused pale-green immature fruit and lower chlorophyll. 2. In HO carrying glk2/psy1, PRR2 silencing caused pale-green immature fruit and peach ripe fruit with reduced chloroplast size, thylakoids, chlorophylls, and carotenoids. 3. PRR2 physically interacted with GLK2 in Y2H and BiFC, and the GCT box was required for interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association analysis in accessions; F2 segregation/genotyping; VIGS silencing; TEM plastid ultrastructure; HPLC pigment profiling; qRT-PCR; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low expression reported in pepper and tomato compared with CaGLK2 according to authors
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit-associated but relatively low transcript abundance; spatial role in fruits
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: SPME-GC-MS volatile profiling; HPLC pigment profiling; transcript comparison using published pepper/tomato datasets; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing PRR2 decreases pigment nutritional quality and changes flavor volatiles in sensitized genotypes; phenotypic effect is background dependent.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be considered jointly with GLK2 and key carotenoid genes for breeding predictable immature and ripe fruit color and nutritional quality.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors emphasize PRR2 has weaker standalone effect than GLK2 but is required with GLK2 for full pigmentation control.
